title: Muur Talisman

{{Quote|Karness Muur requested I create this, but it shall be my triumph. My experimentation with the Heart of Graush and my alchemic mastery of raising Sithspawn will culminate in the first Great Amulet to be worn by one of the Lords of the Sith.|Sorzus Syn|<ref name="Book of Sith" />}}
The '''Muur Talisman''' was the first of the three [[Great Amulet]]s created by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Sorzus Syn]]. Designed for her fellow [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Karness Muur]], the [[Sith talisman/Legends|Talisman]] had the ability to turn other beings into mindless thralls that were at the possessor's command. It was a powerful object of great antiquity and was mentioned in many [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] dating from before the [[Golden Age of the Sith]]. At some time the Talisman was buried in the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]] of the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] until [[3965 BBY]] when the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Pulsipher]] lead an excavation party to retrieve it. Upon its exposure, the Talisman latched onto [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Celeste Morne]] and began to corrupt her before [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] sealed her and the Talisman in [[Dreypa's Oubliette]] where they both remained for about 4,000 years until [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].

==History==
===Origins===
{{Quote|Though Karness will receive the first, I will complete more than one amulet. The second will go to Dreypa, so those two fools can battle each other unceasingly. The third shall be far stronger than the others, and it shall be mine alone.|Sorzus Syn|<ref name="Book of Sith" />}}
[[File:Pointyearedman.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The talisman's first owner, Karness Muur.]]
The [[Sith alchemist/Legends|Sith alchemist]] [[Sorzus Syn]] created the Muur Talisman for the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Karness Muur]], to enable him to create [[rakghoul]]s and to sustain his essence, granting him spiritual immortality.<ref name="Book of Sith" /> When his physical body died, the Talisman would seek out the most powerful Force user to act as his vessel and conduit. For centuries the Muur Talisman remained in obscurity. Many Sith Lords—including [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]—tore at each other to find it and claim its power for themselves, but the Talisman was never found, as if the Force itself did not want it found. At some point, its last owner had been crushed in a cave-in in the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] where the talisman remained for at least hundreds of years, spreading the [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|rakghoul plague]] amongst the Outcasts. As of [[3963 BBY]] the [[Jedi Covenant]] speculated that the Talisman could be located in the Taris's Undercity. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Xamar]] of the [[First WatchCircle]] was attempting to locate the artifact when they were forced to leave the planet following [[Zayne Carrick]]'s escape.<ref name="KotOR25" />

===Rediscovery===
Later, after the [[Taris Siege|Mandalorian invasion of Taris]], the Covenant witnessed a vision of impending doom related directly to the talisman and Zayne, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Cade Skywalker]]. [[Celeste Morne]] was sent to continue the search on Taris, but arrived just in time to witness a Mandalorian excavation party led by [[Pulsipher]] retrieve the talisman.<ref name="KotOR25" />

Pulsipher headed for [[Jebble]] and before he landed, he opened the case holding the Talisman, under the hidden eyes of Celeste Morne, [[Zayne Carrick]] and [[Marn Hierogryph]], stowaways on Pulsipher's ship, the ''[[Mar'eyce]]''. Pulsipher stated his theories that the key to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] are the objects, not the people and planned on unlocking the device's power to negate the Jedi's powers to further their victory. Celeste tried to [[Mind trick/Legends|make]] him throw it out the airlock, but under the Talisman's influence he was able to counter her mind trick. It attached to Pulsipher's arm and it began controlling him, even turning him on a fellow Mandalorian, leaving some glowing red marks on his comrade's armor chest plates. Those marks later turned the Mandalorian into a rakghoul and the plague spread throughout the Mandalorian ranks. The rakghoul plague spread faster than usual, transforming the victims into smarter, organized rakghouls, wearing Neo-Crusader armor and using weapons against Zayne and Celeste as the two Jedi attempted to stop the plague and warn the Mandalorians and Republic of the threat.<ref name="KotOR26">[[Knights of the Old Republic 26|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 26]]</ref>

When Pulsipher captured Zayne to interrogate him for the Talisman's secrets, it sensed the Padawan's [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]]. The Talisman detached from the Mandalorian, turned the rakghouls on Pulsipher, and attempted to attach itself to Zayne. As Celeste struggled to pry off the Talisman to save Zayne she called out to the talisman's attention by telling it she was a Jedi. Immediately, the Talisman attached itself to her neck. In that moment Celeste saw Muur's spirit appear where he told her that he was the future and she was his. Celeste then discovered that the Talisman gave her the ability to control the rakghouls.<ref name="KotOR25" />

===A suitable host===
{{Quote|I can feel the change inside me. Please, Zayne…''end it now''.|Celeste Morne|<ref name="Knights of the Old Republic 28">[[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 28]]</ref>}}
Celeste began to fall under the Talisman's evil corruption and sensed the plague's immense potential along with the presence of the spirit of Karness Muur in the Talisman. She pleaded with Zayne to end her life while she could still control herself, but the rogue Padawan had a better idea. He enclosed Celeste in an [[Dreypa's Oubliette|oubliette of the Sith Dreypa]], hoping to bring her to Covenant researchers on [[Odryn]] to remove the Talisman. However, [[Cassus Fett]] bombarded Jebble with [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear missiles]] and Zayne presumed Celeste dead and the Talisman destroyed.<ref name="KotOR28" /> The Jedi Covenant assumed Zayne was corrupted by the Talisman and killed Celeste.<ref name="KotOR30">[[Knights of the Old Republic 30|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 30]]</ref>

===Copies===
When Zayne and his friends journeyed to the planet Odryn, [[Jarael]], disguised as Celeste, and the [[Moomo Brothers]] pretended to carry the Muur Talisman, in order for them, Zayne, and Gryph to gain entrance to the [[Sanctum of the Exalted]].<ref name="Star Wars Knights of the Old Republic 29: Exalted, Part 1">[[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]]</ref>

A replica of the Talisman containing Zayne's lightsaber was crafted to improve on the image that Zayne was a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] when Xamar brought him into the [[Draay Estate]]. However, [[Haazen]] immediately saw through the charade.

===Four millennia later===
{{Quote|Yes! Kill her! Take my Talisman for your own! You ''sense'' that power that could be yours. ''Take it!''<nowiki></nowiki>|Karness Muur to Darth Vader|<ref name="Dark Times 12">[[Dark Times 12|''Dark Times'' 12]]</ref>}}
By [[1,400 BBY]], the oubliette was found by ice miners of Jebble. It was passed down by many owners, through blood and trade before it came into the hands of the crew of the ''[[Uhumele]]'' who were willing to trade it. But the Talisman and its wearer were freed from stasis by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Darth Vader who was looking for a way to overthrow the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]]. But Celeste Morne attacked Vader and, using the power of the Talisman, turned his [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] into rakghouls, forcing Vader to escape and leave Celeste along with rakghouls and the spirit of Karness Muur on the [[Unidentified desert moon|unknown moon]].<ref name="Star Wars: Dark Times: Vector">''[[Star Wars: Dark Times: Vector]]''</ref>

===Another century later===
[[File:ShatteredTalisman.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Cade Skywalker using the [[Shatterpoint/Legends|shatterpoint]] technique to destroy the Talisman.]]
The Talisman had stayed on Celeste's neck for a very long time, even right into her trip into the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. She was determined to make sure that the Talisman possessed no one else. Thus, she eventually used its powers once again when she joined [[Cade Skywalker]]'s entourage to kill [[Darth Krayt]]. She turned many Imperials at the [[Had Abbadon]] base into rakghouls to act as her bodyguard while they waited. Muur, in the meantime, played with the minds of her companions, giving [[Shado Vao]] a vision of him slaying Celeste and the Talisman possessing him. [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] desired the Talisman to further his goals but a few of his Knights were losing faith in him. Soon, Muur's whispering annoyed Cade to the point where he threatens to send Celeste and the Talisman into a black hole.

====Destruction====
Krayt took the bait, and traveled to Had Abbadon with his most trusted Sith Lords. The resulting [[Battle on Had Abbadon|battle]] saw the mortal wounding of Darth Krayt and the death of Celeste Morne. With Morne dead, the Talisman attached itself to Cade. But Cade proved to be above its influence, and used the Force to destroy it and Muur forever resulting in the extinction of the rakghouls and the end of the rakghoul plague.

==Abilities==
[[File:MuurTalisman-SWKOTOR26.jpg|thumb|left|The Muur Talisman being held]]
{{Quote|This Talisman… it could turn our enemy's armies into monstrous warriors that will obey ''me''. Yes. I want that.|[[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]|<ref name="Legacy (2006) 29">[[Legacy (2006) 29|''Legacy'' (2006) 29]]</ref>}}
Through use of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]], any non-Force-sensitive that was struck by the Talisman became infected with the rakghoul plague and transformed more quickly than from rakghoul bites in the Under City of Taris.<ref name="KotOR26" /> In addition to that under the command by a Force-user the Talisman could transform into rakghouls all suitable beings in much wider radius.<ref name="DarkTimes12">[[Dark Times 12|''Dark Times'' 12]]</ref>

The power of the Talisman also allowed its claws to become legs for mobility and bound itself to anyone who called to it.<ref name="KotOR25" /> The Talisman also stopped all aging in its host,<ref name="Jedi Academy Training Manual">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref> as exemplified by the lack of aging on the part of Celeste Morne from 19 BBY to [[137 ABY]]. But the greatest secret concerning the Talisman was that it housed the spirit of Karness Muur, who could speak to nearby Force-sensitives and appear as an apparition to whomever was possessed by the Talisman.<ref name="KotOR28" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Muur Talisman is also the logo for the [[Star Wars: Vector|Vector]] metaseries. However, starting with the ''Dark Times'' section of the metaseries and continuing to [[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Legacy'' (2006) 28]], the logo was printed upside down for all issues. It is likely this was not a coincidence.

In ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', the Muur Talisman is obtainable through the [[Champions of the Force (SWGTCG)|''Champions of the Force'']] trading card game as a loot card. When used, it activates a buff.<ref name="SWG">{{GalaxiesNGE}}</ref><ref name="SWGTCG">{{SWGTCG|set=Champions of the Force|cardname=Muur Talisman|swglink=Muur talisman.png}}</ref> This should, however, be considered non-canon, as the Talisman never left Celeste Morne.
